steps.title: The Queen box-in
steps.point: With King + Queen vs a lone King, never rush. Use the queen a knight's-move away from the enemy king to shrink its box without stalemating, and walk your own king up to help.

steps.title: Queen + King checkmate
steps.point: This is the goal: the queen delivers mate on the back rank while your king guards every escape square. The defending king has no legal move — that is checkmate, not stalemate.

steps.title: The back-rank mate
steps.point: A king trapped behind its own unmoved pawns is vulnerable. Play Ra8+ and it is instant mate — the f7/g7/h7 pawns block every escape. Always give your own king luft (h3/g3) to avoid this.

steps.title: The rook ladder (staircase)
steps.point: King + Rook vs King is won by 'ladder mate'. Use the rook to build a wall one rank/file at a time and drive the enemy king to the edge, then bring your king up to deliver mate.

steps.title: Two-rook 'lawnmower' mate
steps.point: Two rooks mate a lone king without help from your own king. Check with one rook to push the king back a rank, then swing the other rook over — the rooks 'mow' the king to the edge and mate.
